title: The Side of Seed Oils Nobody Talks About

chunks.title: Concerns about Refined Oils
chunks.summary: The discussion highlights concerns regarding the refining, bleaching, and deodorizing processes of oils. Specific emphasis is placed on the potential risks associated with chemical contaminants like benzene, heavy metals, and phthalates that may be present in seed oils.

chunks.title: The Effects of Heating Oils
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to the dangers posed by heating oils, specifically highlighting compounds like acryine that can form when oils are exposed to high heat. The comparison of acryine levels in heated oils to those found in cigarettes indicates significant health risks associated with fried foods.

chunks.title: Alternative Cooking Oils
chunks.summary: The segment discusses alternatives to seed oils, particularly focusing on tallow and its properties. Although cooking with tallow may reduce certain harmful compounds, the speakers caution that it doesn't fully mitigate health risks associated with deep-fried foods.
chunks.transcript: this is the potentially the worst use case of seed oils right >> um that looks to be very bad but um at least in terms of that one compound so there's there's a there's a um a spectrum here >> so what about like uh steak and shake down the road they're advertising that they cook all their fries in tallow now >> so my perspective is this doesn't solve the equation completely if you actually when you rate into that one they they never solve this problem where the fries were pre-oaked in seed oils. >> Oh, no. >> So, they they're cooking them in tallow. The tallow they're cooking them in has been um altered. It's refined. >> So, it has less saturated fats and more monounsaturated fats, so they can work with it more easily.
